stem: A quality control study surveys 25 manufactured units and observes 6 positive outcomes out of 25 (24.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (14 observations, rate = 21.4%) and Group B (11 observations, rate = 27.3%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 38% and 53% of the population. Estimate the true product defect rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.24. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.2526 (range [0.2495, 0.2561]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.115, 0.4343]. Point estimate: 0.2526.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.46: 0.46×0.229 + 0.54×0.2727 = 0.2526.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
